name: AML/CTF & Financial-Sanctions Legal Corpus — Australia (v1)
tagline: A neutral, provenance-first record of Australian anti-money-laundering / counter-terrorism-financing and financial-sanctions law, with the FATF standard as backbone. Every text carries its official source, retrieval date, citation, jurisdiction, legal status, language, an authoritative-status flag, and a SHA-256 hash; every change is a new dated version. A reference record of the law — not legal or compliance advice.

card.description: A neutral, **provenance-first**, machine-readable record of the law of anti-money-laundering / counter-terrorism-financing (AML/CTF) and financial sanctions, anchored on Australia with the FATF 40 Recommendations as backbone. Every record carries its official source, retrieval date, citation, jurisdiction, legal status, language, an authoritative-status flag, and a SHA-256 content hash; every change is a new dated version.
card.license_terms_examples: Commonwealth of Australia legislation under CC BY 4.0; FATF/OECD material under copyright, restricted
card.author: Dan Cheah
card.concepts_note: curated (human-reviewed) for the main instruments, keyword-derived elsewhere; unofficial
